brands.name: Wag N' Wash
brands.notes: Full-line natural pet food, bakery, and self-serve dog-wash retail franchise (founded Colorado Springs, 1999; franchising since 2006). Actively recruiting via franchise.wagnwash.com. Small but growing system (~23 franchised units) with strong per-store sales; the franchisor runs both franchised and company-owned stores. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Very small, young-cohort system (~23 franchised units)
riskFlags.detail: Per the 2025 FDD outlet tables there were only about 23 franchised stores (plus 6 company-owned) at the end of 2024, up from 14 in 2022. Item 19 reflects just 11 reporting franchised stores, so systemwide averages rest on a thin, immature base.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (outlet tables and Item 19)

riskFlags.title: High build cost relative to a sub-$1.4M average store
riskFlags.detail: Item 7 runs $520,520-$1,357,600 for a retail buildout while 11 reporting franchised stores averaged $1,301,435 annual gross sales (bottom half $1,081,795), with labor at ~36.6% and rent ~10.3% of sales - a capital-heavy retail model with thin margins on the low end.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (Item 7 and Item 19)

riskFlags.title: Lawsuit/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note that Wag N' Wash discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/Item 4), which prospective buyers should review directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Wag N Wash Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Wag N Wash Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

franchiseFee.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and SHARPSHEETS)

royaltyPercent.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: Ramping royalty: 2% of gross sales months 1-12, 3% months 13-24, 4% thereafter.

adFundPercent.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
adFundPercent.note: After month 12: lesser of 3.5% of gross sales or $8,333/month (capped $100,000/yr). First 12 months are fixed-dollar: $3,350/month local + $1,000/month national fund.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average annual gross sales across 11 reporting franchised stores (1/1-12/28/2024); top half $1,674,769, bottom half $1,081,795.

totalUnits.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter); Vetted Biz

yearFounded.source: Wag N' Wash brand history (franchise.wagnwash.com); franchising.com
yearFounded.note: First self-serve dog wash with in-house bakery opened in Colorado Springs in 1999.

yearFranchising.source: franchising.com Wag N' Wash overview; brand history

veteranDiscount.source: Wag N' Wash FAQ (wagnwashfranchising.com); IFA VetFran
veteranDiscount.note: 20% off initial franchise fee for honorably discharged veterans (VetFran); 10% off for qualifying first responders.

isGrowing.source: Wag N' Wash 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)

mobileBased.source: Wag N' Wash franchisor site (retail storefront model)

brands.name: EarthWise Pet
brands.notes: Pet-supply, nutrition, and grooming retail franchise (Groom Bar by EarthWise Pet) marketing 200+ locally owned and operated locations. Actively recruiting via earthwisepetfranchise.com. The dominant structural concern is a shrinking franchise base: franchised units have fallen from ~150 to ~107 while the franchisor reacquires stores into company ownership. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Franchised unit count contracting (~150 to ~107)
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows franchised units declining from roughly 150 to 107 while company-owned units rose to ~58 through reacquisitions - a net shrinking franchise pool even as the brand markets '200+ locations,' which include earlier independently owned stores.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS EarthWise Pet FDD analysis; Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Wide performance dispersion (median ~$482K vs top ~$2.2M)
riskFlags.detail: Performance data show a median around $482,000 with top performers reaching ~$2.2M and an average unit volume near $755,000 - a heavy right skew that makes the headline AUV unrepresentative of a typical store.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS EarthWise Pet FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: No litigation or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report EarthWise Pet does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its 2024 FDD - a comparatively clean Item 3/Item 4 record.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / FDD-tracking analyses

totalInvestmentMin.source: FranchisePayback EarthWise Pet FDD data; franchisor site

totalInvestmentMax.source: FranchisePayback EarthWise Pet FDD data; franchisor site

royaltyPercent.source: FranchisePayback EarthWise Pet FDD data; SHARPSHEETS

avgUnitRevenue.source: SHARPSHEETS / FranchisePayback EarthWise Pet FDD-derived AUV
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average unit volume ~$755,000; median sales ~$482,000 with top performers ~$2.2M (heavy right skew).

totalUnits.note: ~108 franchised + ~58 company-owned per 2024 FDD-derived data; franchisor markets 200+ locations (includes legacy independents).

yearFounded.note: Brand traces retail roots to 1977 (Westminster, CA) per franchisor materials; franchising entity dated 2005.

veteranDiscount.note: $5,000 off the franchise fee for veterans.

isGrowing.note: Declining franchised unit count alongside company reacquisitions.

mobileBased.source: EarthWise Pet franchisor site (retail storefront model)

item19Available.source: SHARPSHEETS EarthWise Pet FDD analysis (Item 19-derived figures)

brands.name: Bark Busters Home Dog Training
brands.notes: World's largest in-home dog-training franchise (founded Australia 1989; operating in the US since June 2000), with offices in roughly 40 states. Low-investment, home-based, mobile service model - trainers deliver behavior consultations in clients' homes, so there is no storefront buildout. Franchisor markets offices in 40 states, so state availability is omitted here.

riskFlags.title: High royalty (up to 10%) on a low-revenue, single-operator model
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived sources cite a royalty around 8-10% of gross sales plus a marketing fee - a steep ongoing burden for an owner-operator in-home service business where revenue is constrained by one trainer's billable hours.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS Bark Busters FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Lawsuit/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Bark Busters discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/Item 4), which prospective franchisees should review directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Bark Busters Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Owner-bound, non-scalable service model
riskFlags.detail: Bark Busters franchisees personally deliver in-home training; revenue is tied to one trainer's capacity and brand reputation rather than a scalable multi-employee or multi-location footprint, capping single-territory upside.
riskFlags.source: Bark Busters About Us (franchisor); Franchise Help overview

liquidCapitalMin.note: Some directories cite a lower $20,000 minimum cash figure.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Vetted Biz Bark Busters FDD analysis
totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS cites $71,100-$96,400; Vetted Biz cites $77,900-$117,000 - low-investment home-based model.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Vetted Biz Bark Busters FDD analysis

franchiseFee.source: Vetted Biz Bark Busters FDD analysis

royaltyPercent.source: Vetted Biz Bark Busters FDD analysis
royaltyPercent.note: Sources vary: Vetted Biz cites 10%; SHARPSHEETS cites 8% royalty + 2% marketing.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz Bark Busters FDD analysis
totalUnits.note: US units; SHARPSHEETS cites ~135 US. Franchisor markets 350+ locations across 6 countries.

yearFounded.note: Founded in Australia in 1989 by Sylvia and Danny Wilson.

yearFranchising.source: Bark Busters About Us (operating in the US since June 2000)
yearFranchising.note: Vetted Biz lists 2013 as the US franchising entity date; brand has operated/franchised in the US since 2000.

veteranDiscount.source: Bark Busters USA (Military Friendly Franchise recognition)
veteranDiscount.note: VetFran participant; specific discount amount not published.

businessModelType.source: Bark Busters franchisor site (in-home consumer dog training)

mobileBased.source: Bark Busters franchisor site (home-based, in-home training)

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz; SHARPSHEETS Bark Busters FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS (no systemwide AUV located in FDD summaries)

brands.name: Dog Training Elite
brands.notes: Mobile, in-home dog-training franchise (obedience, therapy, service, and protection dog training) founded by Marine veteran John Mestas, franchising from Lehi, UT since 2015. Explosive recent growth to ~395 franchised units (all franchised, zero company-owned) and a 2024 Inc. 5000 honoree. Mobile/territory model with no storefront. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Hyper-growth from a small base: 57 to 395 units in ~3 years
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units jumped from 57 (2020) to 146 (2021), 365 (2022), and 395 (2023) - a +219-unit single-year leap in 2022. Most of the system is very young, so systemwide averages rest on largely unseasoned territories.
riskFlags.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: Extreme performance dispersion across territories
riskFlags.detail: 2023 Item 19: the top 5 franchisees averaged $977,911 while the middle 53 averaged $253,954 and the bottom 5 averaged just $39,110 - a wide spread on a sub-$300K systemwide average, signaling that outcomes depend heavily on the operator and territory.
riskFlags.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: Large multi-territory franchise fee ($110,000 for 3 territories)
riskFlags.detail: The initial fee of $110,000 buys 3 territories (additional territories $30,000-$40,000 each), committing a new owner to a larger geographic footprint than a single market - a structural push toward multi-territory development at signing.
riskFlags.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter)
franchiseFee.note: $110,000 for 3 territories; additional territories $40,000 (1st), $35,000 (2nd), $30,000 each thereafter.

royaltyPercent.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter)

adFundPercent.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter)
adFundPercent.note: 1% brand fund plus $500/month online advertising/SEO. SHARPSHEETS/Entrepreneur summaries cite a 2% national marketing fee.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Vetted Biz)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales; the 2024 FDD-derived middle 53 franchisees averaged $253,954 (median $229,801), with top 5 at $977,911 and bottom 5 at $39,110.

totalUnits.source: Dog Training Elite 2024 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)
totalUnits.note: All 395 franchised; 0 company-owned at end of 2023.

yearFounded.note: Founded by Marine veteran John Mestas; some sources cite 2009/2011 for the training business.

financingOffered.note: Third-party financing relationships referenced; direct vs third-party not specified.

brands.name: Sit Means Sit Dog Training
brands.notes: One of the largest US-based dog-training franchises (founded by Fred Hassen in Paradise/Las Vegas, NV in 1998; franchising since January 2009), with ~163 US locations. Low-investment, predominantly in-home/mobile training model with optional training-center facilities. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: High royalty (8-9%) on a low-revenue owner-operator model
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived sources cite a royalty of roughly 8-9% of sales plus an advertising fee - a heavy ongoing burden on a low-investment dog-training business whose revenue is tied to a single trainer's billable capacity.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Sit Means Sit FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Lawsuit/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Sit Means Sit discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/Item 4), which prospective franchisees should review directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Sit Means Sit Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: No systemwide Item 19 AUV located in FDD summaries
riskFlags.detail: Public FDD summaries for Sit Means Sit do not surface a systemwide average-unit-revenue figure, leaving prospective buyers without a clear earnings benchmark and reliant on franchisee validation calls.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Sit Means Sit FDD analyses

brands.name: The Dog Stop
brands.notes: All-in-one pet-care franchise (dog daycare, overnight boarding, grooming, training, in-home care, and retail) founded in Pittsburgh, PA in 2009; franchising since 2014. Fast-growing but still small (~37 units), capital-intensive facility model. Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Labor-heavy economics on a high build cost
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows average gross sales of ~$917,252 but labor running ~48.6% of sales against an Item 7 buildout of $553,500-$1,112,800 - a capital-intensive, staffing-heavy facility model where margin compression is a real risk.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS The Dog Stop FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Very small, young system (~37 units)
riskFlags.detail: The system totals roughly 37 units (about 31 franchised + 6 company-owned) as of 2024 - up from 25 the prior year. Rapid growth off a small base means long-run unit economics rest on a thin cohort of mature facilities.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz The Dog Stop FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: High capital requirements ($350K liquid / $750K net worth)
riskFlags.detail: Prospective franchisees must show ~$350,000 liquid capital and ~$750,000 net worth for a single facility, a steep bar relative to the system's small footprint and limited mature-unit track record.
riskFlags.source: FranNet / Franchise Gator The Dog Stop listings

totalUnits.note: ~31 franchised + 6 company-owned as of 2024 (up from 25 the prior year).

brands.name: Waxing the City
brands.notes: Body-waxing studio franchise owned by Purpose Brands (formerly Self Esteem Brands, parent of Anytime Fitness), with 151 franchised studios and zero company-owned units as of 12/31/2024. Membership-driven recurring-revenue model ('Club Orange'). Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Wide performance dispersion and a sub-$515K average
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 across 134 operating studios: average gross revenue $514,339 (median $488,739) with a range from $73,049 to $1,298,091 - and a stated 18-24 month break-even - so a meaningful share of studios sit well below the average against $311K-$646K buildout costs.
riskFlags.source: Waxing the City 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Unit growth has stalled (+1 net franchised unit in 2024)
riskFlags.detail: Franchised studios grew +15 (2022) and +17 (2023) but added just +1 net in 2024 to reach 151, while company-owned units went to zero - a sharp deceleration that signals saturation or development friction for a single-service beauty concept.
riskFlags.source: Waxing the City 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Stacked marketing obligations on top of royalty
riskFlags.detail: Beyond a 6% royalty (or $100/week minimum), franchisees pay a 2% marketing fund plus a fixed $1,500/month local advertising spend - fixed obligations that weigh on studios performing below the system average.
riskFlags.source: Waxing the City 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing fund plus a fixed $1,500/month local advertising spend.

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross revenue across 134 operating studios; median $488,739; range $73,049-$1,298,091.

brands.name: Sugaring NYC
brands.notes: All-natural sugaring hair-removal studio franchise (founded 2015; franchising since 2017) with 140+ locations open across 26 states. Lower-investment beauty-studio model than wax competitors. Franchisor markets locations in 26 states but does not publish a definitive state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Modest average sales and a long payback (4-6 years)
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows average yearly gross sales of ~$377,306 with estimated owner-operator earnings of $52,823-$67,915 and a payback period of roughly 4.1-6.1 years - a slow return for a beauty-studio concept.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz Sugaring NYC FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Lawsuit/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Sugaring NYC discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/Item 4), which prospective franchisees should review directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Sugaring NYC Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Franchisor reacquiring units into company ownership
riskFlags.detail: Of ~132 total units, about 8 are company-owned per 2025 FDD-derived data - a small but notable corporate footprint to monitor for whether the franchisor is buying back or operating studios directly.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / FranchisePayback Sugaring NYC FDD data

brands.name: Deka Lash
brands.notes: Eyelash-extension studio franchise (DL Franchising, LLC; McMurray, PA), franchising since January 2016 with ~130 franchised units and zero company-owned as of end-2023. Membership-driven recurring-revenue model (memberships ~56% of revenue). No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Steep performance dispersion across thirds
riskFlags.detail: 2023 Item 19 across 109 reporting studios: the top third averaged $495,184 gross revenue while the bottom third averaged just $152,349 - a 3x+ spread on a system whose middle third averaged $287,860, against buildout costs of $285,900-$449,350.
riskFlags.source: Deka Lash 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Heavy stacked marketing obligations plus escalating minimum royalty
riskFlags.detail: Beyond a 6% royalty (rising to a $1,000/month minimum after year 2), franchisees owe a 3% marketing fund (min $500/month after year 1) plus a fixed $2,000/month local marketing requirement - large fixed costs that pressure below-average studios.
riskFlags.source: Deka Lash 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Unit growth decelerated and company units went to zero
riskFlags.detail: Franchised additions slowed from +20 (2022) to +7 (2023) to reach 130, while the franchisor's company-owned studios dropped from 6 to 0 in 2023 - a pullback from corporate operation worth monitoring.
riskFlags.source: Deka Lash 2024 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Deka Lash 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Includes $82,600 paid to the franchisor or affiliate. 2025 FDD-derived sources cite up to $460,850.

brands.name: Frenchies Modern Nail Care
brands.notes: Upscale, sanitation-focused nail-salon franchise (founded Colorado Springs, 2014; franchising since 2017), backed by private-equity firm The Riverside Company. Currently a small operating system (~24-25 open salons) despite having sold 100+ territories years ago - prospective buyers should weigh the gap between sold territories and actually-open units. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Large gap between territories sold and salons actually open
riskFlags.detail: Frenchies announced a '100 territory milestone' in 2019, but only roughly 24-25 salons were open as of 2025-2026 - a wide gap between development commitments and operating units that signals slow openings and/or closures.
riskFlags.source: Franchising.com (100-territory milestone, 2019); xmap.ai location count (2026)

riskFlags.title: High build cost for a small, unproven-at-scale system
riskFlags.detail: The 2026 FDD Item 7 runs roughly $452,000-$550,060 for a single salon, a substantial buildout against a system of only ~24 open units, so systemwide economics rest on a thin operating base.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Frenchies FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Private-equity ownership (The Riverside Company)
riskFlags.detail: Frenchies is a Riverside Company platform investment; PE ownership steers development priorities and an eventual exit, and franchisees are part of a portfolio's return timeline rather than a founder-led system.
riskFlags.source: The Riverside Company press release

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2026 FDD Item 7 cited as $472,883-$550,060; earlier FDD versions $452,000-$507,000.

totalUnits.note: ~25 open salons; ~24 in the US as of January 2026 per xmap.ai.

isGrowing.source: Franchising.com (100 territories sold by 2019) vs ~24 open (2026)
isGrowing.note: Few open units relative to territories sold; net unit trajectory unclear.

fddAvailable.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Frenchies FDD analyses

brands.name: Fantastic Sams
brands.notes: Long-established value hair-salon franchise (founded 1974; franchising since 1976), owned by Dessange International, with 512 franchised salons and zero company-owned. Still actively franchising, but the headline structural fact is a multi-year, one-in-three contraction of the system. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: System has shed ~33% of units since 2022
riskFlags.detail: Salon count fell from 755 (start of 2022) to 512 (end of 2024) - net losses of -54 (2022), -103 (2023), and -86 (2024), with just 7 openings versus 60 closures in 2024 - one of the more visibly contracting brands in personal services.
riskFlags.source: Fantastic Sams 2025 FDD outlet tables (via FranchiseLens / industry analysis)

riskFlags.title: Modest revenue and a 5-7 year payback
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows median annual gross revenue around $299K-$316K (363 reporting units), estimated owner-operator earnings of $44,289-$56,943, and a payback period of roughly 5.2-7.2 years - thin economics for a buildout that can reach ~$462K.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS Fantastic Sams FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Lawsuit/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Fantastic Sams discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/Item 4), which prospective franchisees should review directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Fantastic Sams Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.note: Vetted Biz cites a lower $40,000 minimum cash figure.

royaltyPercent.note: Royalty stated as ~$362/week or 6% of gross sales; marketing/advertising ~$146/week (~3-5% of sales).

adFundPercent.note: Advertising paid as a fixed ~$146/week; percentage equivalent varies by sales volume.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz Fantastic Sams FDD analysis (Item 19-derived)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Median annual gross sales; SHARPSHEETS cites a $299K median across 363 reporting units.

totalUnits.source: Fantastic Sams 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS)
totalUnits.note: All 512 franchised; 0 company-owned (down from 755 at start of 2022).

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz Fantastic Sams FDD analysis

isGrowing.source: Fantastic Sams 2025 FDD outlet tables (755 -> 512 units, 2022-2024)

mobileBased.source: Fantastic Sams franchisor site (salon storefront model)

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz; SHARPSHEETS Fantastic Sams FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS Fantastic Sams FDD analyses

brands.name: Roosters Men's Grooming Centers
brands.notes: Upscale men's grooming/barbershop franchise (haircuts, hot-lather shaves, color) founded 1999; franchising since 2002, owned by Regis Corporation (Minneapolis). Small system (~70-82 units) that has been contracting per recent FDD-derived data. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Declining unit count (~-16% per recent FDD data)
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows the system at roughly 70 units (69 franchised + 1 company-owned) with about a -16% unit trend, down from ~82 the prior year - net contraction for a small men's-grooming concept.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback Roosters FDD data

riskFlags.title: Owned by financially pressured parent Regis Corporation
riskFlags.detail: Roosters sits inside Regis Corporation, the salon operator (Supercuts, SmartStyle) that has navigated heavy debt loads and restructuring in recent years; parent-level financial pressure can affect brand investment and support for a small portfolio brand.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback Roosters FDD data (parent: Regis Corporation); Franchise Direct

riskFlags.title: No franchisor financing offered
riskFlags.detail: Per FDD summaries, neither the franchisor nor any agent or affiliate offers direct or indirect financing to franchisees or guarantees their notes, leases, or obligations - buyers must arrange all capital independently.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Gator Roosters listing (FDD summary)

avgUnitRevenue.note: Estimated AUV; franchisor does not formally disclose a systemwide Item 19 AUV.

totalUnits.source: FranchisePayback / Franchise Gator Roosters FDD data
totalUnits.note: ~82 units (all/nearly all franchised) per 2024 data; ~70 (69 franchised + 1 company) per 2026 FDD-derived figures.

financingOffered.note: No direct or indirect franchisor financing.

isGrowing.source: FranchisePayback Roosters FDD data (~-16% unit trend)

fddAvailable.source: FranchisePayback / Franchise Direct Roosters FDD data

item19Available.source: FranchisePayback (franchisor does not formally disclose AUV)

brands.name: Diesel Barbershop
brands.notes: Industrial-themed men's barbershop franchise (founded San Antonio, TX, 2017; franchising since 2017) with ~31 US franchised locations and a clean FDD litigation record. Young, small system with modest per-unit revenue and a notably long payback. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Very long payback (13.5-15.5 years) on modest revenue
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows estimated owner-operator earnings of $29,002-$37,288 and a payback period of roughly 13.5-15.5 years against a $360,550-$503,050 buildout - an unusually slow return that magnifies the risk if a unit underperforms.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz Diesel Barbershop FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: High royalty (7.5-8%) on a low-AUV concept
riskFlags.detail: Sources cite a royalty of roughly 7.5-8% of gross sales plus a marketing fee, while FDD-derived AUV runs only ~$207K-$338K - a heavy ongoing fee load relative to per-unit revenue.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Diesel Barbershop FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: No litigation or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report Diesel Barbershop does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its FDD - a comparatively clean Item 3/Item 4 record for the brand.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz Diesel Barbershop FDD analysis

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz Diesel Barbershop FDD analysis (minimum cash)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Diesel Barbershop FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and Vetted Biz)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Diesel Barbershop FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.source: Diesel Barbershop FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Vetted Biz)

royaltyPercent.note: SHARPSHEETS cites 7.5% of gross sales; Vetted Biz cites 8%. Plus a 1-2% advertising/marketing fee.

adFundPercent.note: SHARPSHEETS cites 2% advertising; Vetted Biz cites 1%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: SHARPSHEETS Diesel Barbershop FDD Item 19 (2024 FDD, 2023 data)
avgUnitRevenue.note: SHARPSHEETS cites ~$338,000 AUV across 25 franchisees + 4 affiliates; Vetted Biz cites a lower ~$207,153.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz Diesel Barbershop FDD analysis
totalUnits.note: ~31 US franchised; franchisor cites 32+ locations and continuing expansion.

brands.name: V's Barbershop
brands.notes: Upscale, old-fashioned men's barbershop franchise (haircuts, hot-lather shaves, shoeshine) founded by Jim 'V' Valenzuela in Phoenix, AZ in 1999. ~58-60 franchised units, all franchised, with strong, steadily growing per-unit sales and a declining-scale royalty that rewards higher-volume shops. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue range and slow, steady (not explosive) growth
riskFlags.detail: 2023 Item 19 (47 units open 3+ years): average $542,750, median $519,529, range $170,098-$1,134,269, with only 45% of units meeting or exceeding the average - a meaningful tail of lower-volume shops. Unit growth has been modest (+2/+2/+3 across 2021-2023).
riskFlags.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: High build cost relative to a single-shop service model
riskFlags.detail: Item 7 runs $326,500-$602,450 (some directories cite up to $631,000) for a single barbershop, a substantial buildout for a labor-bound service concept where revenue is capped by chair count and stylist capacity.
riskFlags.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter and SHARPSHEETS)

riskFlags.title: Front-loaded royalty (6% up to $500K, then 3.5%)
riskFlags.detail: The royalty is 6% of gross sales up to $500,000 and 3.5% above that, so newer or lower-volume shops pay the higher rate on most or all of their revenue - the relief only materializes once a shop scales past $500K.
riskFlags.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

totalInvestmentMin.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Some directories cite a wider $265,000-$631,000 range.

totalInvestmentMax.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter)

royaltyPercent.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)
royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales up to $500,000; 3.5% above $500,000.

adFundPercent.note: Marketing/advertising contribution between 1% and 3% of gross sales (national fund 2%).

avgUnitRevenue.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2023 average gross sales for 47 units open 3+ years; median $519,529; range $170,098-$1,134,269.

totalUnits.source: SHARPSHEETS / FranchisePayback V's Barbershop FDD data
totalUnits.note: ~58 franchised at end of 2023 (per Franchise Chatter outlet tables) growing toward ~60; all franchised, 0 company-owned.

yearFounded.source: V's Barbershop brand history (vbarbershop.com); Franchise Gator
yearFounded.note: Founded by Jim 'V' Valenzuela in Phoenix, AZ in November 1999.

yearFranchising.source: Franchise Gator / Franchise Help V's Barbershop listings
yearFranchising.note: Franchising began in the mid-2000s; exact year not pinned across sources (~2005-2006).

veteranDiscount.note: 10% off the initial franchise fee for veterans (a $3,000 discount).

isGrowing.source: V's Barbershop 2024 FDD outlet tables (via Vetted Biz)

mobileBased.source: V's Barbershop franchisor site (barbershop storefront model)

brands.name: CycleBar
brands.notes: Largest US network of premium indoor-cycling studios (founded 2004), now owned by Extraordinary Brands, which acquired CycleBar and Rumble Boxing from Xponential Fitness in August 2025. Membership-driven recurring-revenue model. The system has been contracting since 2022, and prospective buyers should weigh that decline against the franchisor's growth pitch. No public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: System contracting since 2022 (248 to 216 franchised units)
riskFlags.detail: Franchised studios fell from 248 (end of 2022) to 216 (end of 2024) - net losses of -32 (2023) and -27 (2024) - while company-owned units went to zero, a multi-year decline for a brand that markets itself as a growth opportunity.
riskFlags.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Extreme revenue dispersion and high attrition
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 across 183 studios: average gross revenue $424,125 (median $406,514) with a range of $29,307-$1,282,643; the bottom quartile averaged just $192,302 vs $709,117 for the top quartile, with average monthly membership attrition of 7.7% - a churn-sensitive model.
riskFlags.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Ownership change in 2025 (Xponential to Extraordinary Brands)
riskFlags.detail: Extraordinary Brands acquired CycleBar (and Rumble Boxing) from Xponential Fitness in August 2025; ownership transitions can shift development priorities, support, and supplier relationships for existing franchisees.
riskFlags.source: PRNewswire / FranchiseWire (Extraordinary Brands acquisition)

totalInvestmentMin.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Includes $193,050-$292,350 paid to the franchisor or affiliates.

totalInvestmentMax.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand development fund plus a local advertising requirement of the greater of $1,500 or 2% of prior-month gross sales.

avgUnitRevenue.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross revenue across 183 franchised studios; median $406,514; range $29,307-$1,282,643.

totalUnits.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: 216 franchised + 0 company-owned at end of 2024 (down from 248 franchised in 2022).

yearFranchising.source: Franchise Times Top 400 (2025); CycleBar franchise materials
yearFranchising.note: Some sources cite 2014; Xponential acquired the brand in 2017.

isGrowing.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD outlet tables (248 -> 216 franchised units, 2022-2024)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Membership-driven recurring revenue (140-434 average monthly active members by quartile; ~7.7% average monthly attrition)
customerAcquisitionModel.source: CycleBar 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
